mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4% signals a weakening labor market that reduces aggregate demand for equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 108 signals a strengthening demand for goods that increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 108 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below 1.2 million signals a weakening supply of new homes that reduces aggregate demand for equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST < 1.2M are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in the CPI above 250 signals a monetary easing cycle that increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in periods with both FEDFUNDS decline and CPIAUCSL increase are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial jobless claims above 200 thousand signals a weakening labor market that reduces aggregate demand for equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 200K are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real personal spending above 12.5 million signals a strengthening demand for goods that increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 12.5M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 105 signals a weakening supply of goods that reduces aggregate demand for equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 105 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
